destinations.evidence: The verifier enforces inclusive TTL bounds, rejects unsupported precompile metadata, and rejects zero executor addresses before accepting an async commitment as structurally valid.
After structural checks, the verifier validates and decodes the precompile payload and rejects executor or TTL drift between the payload and commitment fields.

equivalence_argument: The verifier rejects zero executor addresses after TTL and precompile metadata validation and before payload parity, simulation, and executor registry checks. Invalid TTL and unsupported precompile addresses are rejected in the same structural validation function.
notes: The rejection is an InvalidAsyncCommitment error string in the port rather than the old standalone ZeroExecutorAddress enum.
rationale: The merge adds ZeroExecutorAddress as a validation error and tests it as both a unit and property-based invariant to avoid inconsistent fee recipient and executor lookup behavior. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async commitment structural validation must reject commitments whose executor_address is the zero address, in addition to rejecting invalid TTLs and unsupported precompile addresses.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct TxAsyncCommitment values with executor_address zero and non-zero while keeping TTL/precompile valid; assert validate_commitment_structure rejects only the zero executor case with ZeroExecutorAddress.
summary: Async commitments require a nonzero executor address.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct TxAsyncCommitment values with executor_address zero and non-zero while keeping TTL/precompile valid; assert validate_commitment_structure rejects only the zero executor case with ZeroExecutorAddress.
title: Async commitments require executor address
